tweets.text:
People prefer paying in fiat stables because it's widely accepted. The problem with fixed-issuance assets like Bitcoin have is that you cannot print more to incentivize a new generation or demographic to use it. Because USD is constantly being printed, there is a constant pressure to continue spending the currency and so it flows around and creates an economy. You also presumably have lower fees, faster confirmation times etc. with stables, which matters a lot at lower income levels. I am certain some do also use Bitcoin, but compared to their local currency, both do a good job of preserving purchasing power and the USD does it without downside risk.

It's very early days for Umbra, this has not happened to my knowledge, but I did think it was worth mentioning.

tweets.text: What Ansem is doing with his token probably reinvents how airdrops are done forever. In hindsight it was obvious,  but the idea that you have an evergreen recurring airdrop is actually the way to do it and is the highest EV way to distribute tokens.

thread_tweets.text: What Ansem is doing with his token probably reinvents how airdrops are done forever. In hindsight it was obvious,  but the idea that you have an evergreen recurring airdrop is actually the way to do it and is the highest EV way to distribute tokens.
thread_tweets.full_text: What Ansem is doing with his token probably reinvents how airdrops are done forever. In hindsight it was obvious,  but the idea that you have an evergreen recurring airdrop is actually the way to do it and is the highest EV way to distribute tokens.

thread_tweets.text: Some might say “people have done this” but functionally they haven’t. The core differentiator here is the idea of airdropping new users tokens everyday / week vs multi month seasons which jades users and stagnates momentum.
thread_tweets.full_text: Some might say “people have done this” but functionally they haven’t. The core differentiator here is the idea of airdropping new users tokens everyday / week vs multi month seasons which jades users and stagnates momentum.

thread_tweets.text:
If I was a new token going to launch I would:

Airdrop an initial amount to users -> drip feed a small airdrop everyday / week to new and existing users who are completing tasks such as championing the token or using products in the ecosystem.

tweets.text: On CNBC @steveliesman pointed out that people want health care. Of course they do. People want lots of stuff. But that doesn't mean it's the government's job to provide it. The government's only legitimate role is to stay out of the way so people can pursue that stuff themselves.

tweets.text:
The Trumps made billions selling crypto and shares in Trump-related companies to the public. But their gains did not result from actual value creation, but from the losses suffered by investors who bought the overpriced crypto and shares they sold. Many buyers expected to lose money, as the purchases were disguised bribes. Some likely didn't care if they made money, as they bought small amounts just to show their support. But the majority were likely unsophisticated Trump loyalists, who actually expected to make money on what they believed were legitimate investments. 

My guess is that the Trump Organization will be overwhelmed by lawsuits as victims of these scams seek to recover their losses. It's also likely that the most extreme examples will be exploited by Democrats in highly effective political ads as Republicans will be hard-pressed to defend the conduct.

My guess is that the Trump Organization will be overwhelmed by lawsuits as victims of these scams seek to recover their losses. It's also likely that the most extreme examples will be exploited by Democrats in highly effective political ads as Republicans will be hard-pressed to defend the conduct.
thread_tweets.full_text:
The Trumps made billions selling crypto and shares in Trump-related companies to the public. But their gains did not result from actual value creation, but from the losses suffered by investors who bought the overpriced crypto and shares they sold. Many buyers expected to lose money, as the purchases were disguised bribes. Some likely didn't care if they made money, as they bought small amounts just to show their support. But the majority were likely unsophisticated Trump loyalists, who actually expected to make money on what they believed were legitimate investments. 

My guess is that the Trump Organization will be overwhelmed by lawsuits as victims of these scams seek to recover their losses. It's also likely that the most extreme examples will be exploited by Democrats in highly effective political ads as Republicans will be hard-pressed to defend the conduct.
